Draft a partnership deed under the Indian Partnership Act, 1932 for the firm 'Pioneer Traders' at Surat, business of providing IT consultancy services, between Mr. Salman Gupta, Mrs. Manju Rao. Capital ₹23,00,000 each, profits/losses in equal shares, with arbitration and dissolution clauses.

DEED OF PARTNERSHIP

This Deed of Partnership is made and executed at Surat, Gujarat, on this 6th day of February, 2026,

BY AND BETWEEN
   (a) Mr. Salman Gupta, hereinafter referred to as Partner No. 1;
   (b) Mrs. Manju Rao, hereinafter referred to as Partner No. 2;

The above-named persons are hereinafter collectively referred to as the "Partners" and individually as a "Partner".

WHEREAS the Partners have agreed to carry on business in partnership and are desirous of reducing the terms and conditions of their partnership into writing, in accordance with the provisions of the Indian Partnership Act, 1932.

NOW THIS DEED WITNESSETH AS UNDER:

1. NAME OF THE FIRM — The partnership business shall be carried on in the name and style of "Pioneer Traders" (the "Firm"), or in such other name as the Partners may mutually decide from time to time.

2. PLACE OF BUSINESS — The principal place of business of the Firm shall be at Surat, and/or at such other place or places as the Partners may mutually agree upon.

3. NATURE OF BUSINESS — The business of the Firm shall be that of providing IT consultancy services, and/or any other lawful business as may be mutually agreed upon in writing by the Partners.

4. COMMENCEMENT AND DURATION — The partnership shall commence with effect from the date of these presents and shall be a partnership at will.

5. CAPITAL — The capital of the Firm shall be contributed by the Partners in the sum of ₹23,00,000 (Rupees Twenty Three Lakh only) each, aggregating to ₹46,00,000 (Rupees Forty Six Lakh only), and further capital, if required, shall be brought in by the Partners as may be mutually agreed.

6. PROFITS AND LOSSES — The net profits and losses of the Firm, after providing for all working expenses, shall be divided between and borne by the Partners in equal shares.

7. BANK ACCOUNT — The Firm shall maintain a bank account in the name of the Firm, which shall be operated by such of the Partners as may be authorised by mutual consent.

8. BOOKS OF ACCOUNT — Proper books of account shall be kept at the place of business of the Firm and shall be open to inspection by each Partner; the accounts shall be made up and settled at the close of each financial year.

9. MANAGEMENT — Every Partner shall be just and faithful to the other Partners, shall diligently attend to the business of the Firm, and no Partner shall, without the consent of the others, lend the Firm's money or stand surety for any person on behalf of the Firm.

10. RETIREMENT, DEATH AND INSOLVENCY — On the retirement, death or insolvency of any Partner, the Firm shall not be dissolved but shall be continued by the remaining Partners, who shall pay to the outgoing Partner or his legal heirs the amount standing to his credit, as ascertained from the books of the Firm.

11. DISSOLUTION — In the event of dissolution of the Firm, the assets of the Firm shall be realised and applied, and the surplus, if any, shall be distributed among the Partners in proportion to their respective shares, in accordance with the provisions of the Indian Partnership Act, 1932.

12. ARBITRATION — All disputes and differences arising between the Partners touching the affairs of the Firm or the interpretation of this Deed shall be referred to the arbitration of a sole arbitrator appointed by mutual consent, and the decision of such arbitration shall be final and binding on the Partners.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the Partners have set their respective hands to this Deed of Partnership on the day, month and year first hereinabove written.

____________________
(Mr. Salman Gupta)

____________________
(Mrs. Manju Rao)

WITNESSES:
1. ____________________
2. ____________________

The tenant has carried out unauthorised structural alterations that have damaged the premises. LEGAL NOTICE

Date: [DATE OF NOTICE]
By Registered Post A.D.

To,
Mr. Omkar Naidu,
Flat No. 11, Tulsi Vihar, Gandhi Chowk Road, Andheri West, Nashik 42224.

Subject: Legal notice in respect of unauthorised structural alterations and damage to the said premises, requiring restoration and delivery of vacant possession.

Sir,

Under instructions from and on behalf of my client, Mrs. Vaishali Patil (hereinafter referred to as "my client / the landlord"), I address you the following notice:

1. That my client is the landlord and owner of the premises bearing Flat No. 11, Tulsi Vihar, Gandhi Chowk Road, Andheri West, Nashik 42224 (hereinafter referred to as "the said premises"), which my client let out to you as the tenant thereof at a monthly rent of ₹57,500 (Rupees Fifty-Seven Thousand Five Hundred only).

2. That the tenancy in your favour commenced on 24.10.2023, and at the inception thereof my client received from you an interest-free refundable security deposit of ₹3,45,000 (Rupees Three Lakh Forty-Five Thousand only), being a sum equivalent to six months' rent, the refund whereof is subject to lawful deductions, including towards the cost of making good any damage caused to the said premises.

3. That under the terms of the tenancy you were bound to maintain the said premises in good and tenantable condition and were prohibited from carrying out any structural changes or alterations therein without the prior written consent of my client.

4. That in breach of the said terms, and without any consent or authority of my client, you have carried out unauthorised structural alterations to the said premises, thereby causing material damage to and impairing the structure, fittings and finish of the said premises.

5. That the said unauthorised alterations and the consequent damage constitute a grave breach of the terms of the tenancy, by reason whereof my client is no longer willing to continue the tenancy in your favour.

6. That my client, through me, hereby requires you to restore the said premises to their original condition by making good, at your own cost, all the unauthorised alterations and damage caused by you, and further to quit, vacate and hand over peaceful and vacant possession of the said premises to my client, within 15 (fifteen) days from the date of receipt of this notice. The tenancy in your favour stands terminated under Section 106 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882, upon the expiry of the said period.

7. That should you fail to restore the said premises and to deliver vacant possession within the said period of 15 (fifteen) days, my client shall be constrained to institute a suit for possession and for recovery of the cost of restoration together with mesne profits, entirely at your risk as to costs and consequences.

8. That all the rights and contentions of my client are expressly reserved.

This notice is issued to you by Registered Post A.D., a copy whereof has been retained in my office for record.

Yours faithfully,

____________________
Aishwarya Deshmukh
Advocate for Mrs. Vaishali Patil

Sir,

Under instructions from and on behalf of my client, Mr. Harshad Shah (hereinafter referred to as "my client / the landlord"), I serve upon you this notice:

1. That my client is the landlord and owner of the premises bearing Flat No. 32, Matoshree Heights, Station Road, Andheri West, Nashik 42299 (hereinafter referred to as "the said premises"), let out to you at a monthly rent of Rs. 45,500 (Rupees Forty-Five Thousand Five Hundred only).

2. That the tenancy in your favour commenced on 09.01.2022 upon the terms agreed between the parties, and you were inducted into possession of the said premises as the tenant of my client.

3. That you have, without the consent or authority of my client and in breach of the terms of the tenancy, carried out unauthorised structural alterations to the said premises, thereby causing material damage and impairing the value and condition of the premises.

4. That you are further in arrears of rent for a period of 2 (two) months, which arrears remain due and payable notwithstanding reminders, thereby occasioning a further breach of the tenancy.

5. That my client, through me, hereby terminates the tenancy under Section 106 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882, and calls upon you to restore the said premises to their original condition, to pay the entire arrears of rent, and to quit, vacate and hand over peaceful and vacant possession of the said premises to my client within 30 (thirty) days from the date of receipt of this notice.

Please draft a Memorandum of Understanding between Mrs. Heena Kulkarni of Ahmedabad (First Party) and Ms. Harleen Chauhan of Bhopal (Second Party) for joint development of an e-learning platform. Keep it largely non-binding except confidentiality and dispute resolution, valid for 36 months.

MEMORANDUM OF UNDERSTANDING

This Memorandum of Understanding (hereinafter referred to as this "MOU") is made and entered into on this 12th day of January, 2024, at Ahmedabad,

BY AND BETWEEN

Mrs. Heena Kulkarni, residing at Flat No. 53, Silver Oak Towers, Andheri West, Ahmedabad 237458 (hereinafter referred to as the "First Party", which expression shall, unless repugnant to the context, include his/her heirs, executors, administrators and permitted assigns) of the ONE PART;

AND

Ms. Harleen Chauhan, residing at Flat No. 86, Maple Court, Vashi, Bhopal 522537 (hereinafter referred to as the "Second Party", which expression shall, unless repugnant to the context, include his/her heirs, executors, administrators and permitted assigns) of the OTHER PART.

The First Party and the Second Party are hereinafter individually referred to as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties".

WHEREAS the First Party is engaged in and possesses experience relevant to the proposed collaboration; AND WHEREAS the Second Party desires to associate with the First Party for the purpose of joint development of an e-learning platform; AND WHEREAS the Parties are desirous of recording the broad understanding reached between them by way of this MOU.

NOW, THEREFORE, THE PARTIES HEREBY RECORD THEIR UNDERSTANDING AS UNDER:

1. PURPOSE — The Parties shall, in good faith, explore and undertake the technology collaboration described above and shall extend to each other reasonable cooperation towards its implementation.

2. SCOPE OF COOPERATION — The Parties shall share such information, resources and expertise as may be mutually agreed in writing from time to time, and shall constitute a joint working committee to oversee the activities contemplated herein.

3. COSTS — Save as otherwise agreed in writing, each Party shall bear its own costs and expenses incurred in connection with this MOU.

4. TERM — This MOU shall come into effect from the date first written above and shall, unless extended or terminated earlier in accordance herewith, remain in force for a period of 36 months.

5. CONFIDENTIALITY — Each Party shall keep confidential all proprietary and non-public information disclosed to it by the other Party and shall not disclose the same to any third party without prior written consent, save as required by law. This obligation of confidentiality shall survive the termination of this MOU.

6. BINDING EFFECT — Save and except the clauses relating to Confidentiality, Dispute Resolution and Governing Law, which the Parties intend to be legally binding, this MOU records the present intention of the Parties only and is not intended to create any legally binding obligation on either Party.

7. TERMINATION — Either Party may terminate this MOU by giving thirty (30) days' prior written notice to the other Party, without assigning any reason, and such termination shall be without prejudice to any accrued rights or obligations of the Parties.

8. DISPUTE RESOLUTION — Any dispute or difference arising out of or in connection with this MOU shall, in the first instance, be resolved amicably through mutual negotiation between the Parties, failing which the same shall be referred to and finally resolved by arbitration by a sole arbitrator to be appointed by mutual consent, the seat of arbitration being Ahmedabad.

9. GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION — This MOU shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India, and subject to the arbitration clause aforesaid, the courts at Ahmedabad shall have exclusive jurisdiction.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the Parties have set their respective hands to this MOU on the day, month and year first hereinabove written.

For the First Party                                   For the Second Party


____________________                                  ____________________
(Mrs. Heena Kulkarni)                                                (Ms. Harleen Chauhan)

WITNESSES:
1. ____________________
2. ____________________

CONSUMER COMPLAINT NO. ________ OF 2025

(Complaint under Section 35 of the Consumer Protection Act, 2019)

1. That the Complainant is a "consumer" within the meaning of Section 2(7) of the Consumer Protection Act, 2019, having availed of the goods/services of the Opposite Party for valuable consideration, and the present complaint is filed under Section 35 of the said Act.

2. That on 13.01.2026, the Complainant, for consideration of ₹70,000 (Rupees Seventy Thousand only), availed of the goods/services of the Opposite Party in respect of the matter giving rise to the present complaint.

6. LIMITATION — That the present complaint is filed within the period of two years from the date on which the cause of action arose, and is therefore within limitation as prescribed under Section 69 of the Consumer Protection Act, 2019.

7. JURISDICTION — That this Commission has the territorial jurisdiction to entertain and try the present complaint, as the Complainant resides and the cause of action, wholly or in part, arose within its jurisdiction; and the complaint is also within the pecuniary jurisdiction of this Commission, the value of the goods/services and the compensation claimed not exceeding the limit prescribed.

8. That the Complainant has not filed any other complaint or proceeding in respect of the subject matter of the present complaint before any other forum or authority.

PRAYER

It is, therefore, most respectfully prayed that this Commission may be pleased to:
(a) direct the Opposite Party to refund/pay to the Complainant a sum of ₹1,75,000 (Rupees One Lakh Seventy Five Thousand only) on account of the deficiency in the services aforesaid;
(b) direct the Opposite Party to pay compensation for mental agony and harassment, together with the costs of the present complaint;

MEMORANDUM OF UNDERSTANDING

This Memorandum of Understanding (hereinafter referred to as this "MOU") is made and entered into on this 17th day of November, 2026, at Kanpur,

BY AND BETWEEN

Ms. Simran Banerjee, residing at Flat No. 9, Krishna Apartments, MG Road, Kanpur 558159 (hereinafter referred to as the "First Party", which expression shall, unless repugnant to the context, include his/her heirs, executors, administrators and permitted assigns) of the ONE PART;

AND

Mr. Ajay Sheikh, residing at Flat No. 81, Pearl Residency, Kandivali, Jaipur 283889 (hereinafter referred to as the "Second Party", which expression shall, unless repugnant to the context, include his/her heirs, executors, administrators and permitted assigns) of the OTHER PART.

The First Party and the Second Party are hereinafter individually referred to as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties".

WHEREAS the First Party is engaged in and possesses experience relevant to the proposed collaboration; AND WHEREAS the Second Party desires to associate with the First Party for the purpose of joint development of an e-learning platform; AND WHEREAS the Parties are desirous of recording the broad understanding reached between them by way of this MOU.

NOW, THEREFORE, THE PARTIES HEREBY RECORD THEIR UNDERSTANDING AS UNDER:

1. PURPOSE — The Parties shall, in good faith, explore and undertake the technology collaboration described above and shall extend to each other reasonable cooperation towards its implementation.

2. SCOPE OF COOPERATION — The Parties shall share such information, resources and expertise as may be mutually agreed in writing from time to time, and shall constitute a joint working committee to oversee the activities contemplated herein.

3. COSTS — Save as otherwise agreed in writing, each Party shall bear its own costs and expenses incurred in connection with this MOU.

4. TERM — This MOU shall come into effect from the date first written above and shall, unless extended or terminated earlier in accordance herewith, remain in force for a period of 24 months.

5. CONFIDENTIALITY — Each Party shall keep confidential all proprietary and non-public information disclosed to it by the other Party and shall not disclose the same to any third party without prior written consent, save as required by law. This obligation of confidentiality shall survive the termination of this MOU.

6. BINDING EFFECT — Save and except the clauses relating to Confidentiality, Dispute Resolution and Governing Law, which the Parties intend to be legally binding, this MOU records the present intention of the Parties only and is not intended to create any legally binding obligation on either Party.

7. TERMINATION — Either Party may terminate this MOU by giving thirty (30) days' prior written notice to the other Party, without assigning any reason, and such termination shall be without prejudice to any accrued rights or obligations of the Parties.

8. DISPUTE RESOLUTION — Any dispute or difference arising out of or in connection with this MOU shall, in the first instance, be resolved amicably through mutual negotiation between the Parties, failing which the same shall be referred to and finally resolved by arbitration by a sole arbitrator to be appointed by mutual consent, the seat of arbitration being Kanpur.

9. GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION — This MOU shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India, and subject to the arbitration clause aforesaid, the courts at Kanpur shall have exclusive jurisdiction.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the Parties have set their respective hands to this MOU on the day, month and year first hereinabove written.

For the First Party                                   For the Second Party


____________________                                  ____________________
(Ms. Simran Banerjee)                                                (Mr. Ajay Sheikh)

2. ____________________

BEFORE THE DISTRICT CONSUMER DISPUTES REDRESSAL COMMISSION, HYDERABAD, TELANGANA

CONSUMER COMPLAINT NO. ________ OF 2024

(Complaint under Section 35 of the Consumer Protection Act, 2019)

IN THE MATTER OF:

Mrs. Neha Mishra,
residing at Flat No. 6, Rose Villa, MG Road, Hyderabad 486189
                                                                    ... COMPLAINANT

VERSUS

Crystal Foods Pvt. Ltd.,
[REGISTERED OFFICE / BRANCH ADDRESS]
                                                                    ... OPPOSITE PARTY

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

1. That the Complainant is a "consumer" within the meaning of Section 2(7) of the Consumer Protection Act, 2019, having availed of the goods/services of the Opposite Party for valuable consideration, and the present complaint is filed under Section 35 of the said Act.

2. That on 13.07.2025, the Complainant, for consideration of ₹3,85,000 (Rupees Three Lakh Eighty Five Thousand only), availed of the goods/services of the Opposite Party in respect of the matter giving rise to the present complaint.

3. FACTS OF THE CASE — That the Complainant's grievance arises from defective refrigerator that was never repaired or replaced despite the warranty. Despite repeated requests and reminders, the Opposite Party failed and neglected to redress the grievance of the Complainant.

4. That the aforesaid acts and omissions on the part of the Opposite Party amount to defective goods and have caused the Complainant financial loss, mental agony and harassment, for which the Opposite Party is liable to compensate the Complainant.

5. CAUSE OF ACTION — That the cause of action for the present complaint arose on 13.07.2025 and on subsequent dates when the Opposite Party failed to redress the grievance, and the cause of action is continuing.

6. LIMITATION — That the present complaint is filed within the period of two years from the date on which the cause of action arose, and is therefore within limitation as prescribed under Section 69 of the Consumer Protection Act, 2019.

7. JURISDICTION — That this Commission has the territorial jurisdiction to entertain and try the present complaint, as the Complainant resides and the cause of action, wholly or in part, arose within its jurisdiction; and the complaint is also within the pecuniary jurisdiction of this Commission, the value of the goods/services and the compensation claimed not exceeding the limit prescribed.

8. That the Complainant has not filed any other complaint or proceeding in respect of the subject matter of the present complaint before any other forum or authority.

PRAYER

It is, therefore, most respectfully prayed that this Commission may be pleased to:
(a) direct the Opposite Party to refund/pay to the Complainant a sum of ₹7,85,000 (Rupees Seven Lakh Eighty Five Thousand only) on account of the defects in the goods aforesaid;
(b) direct the Opposite Party to pay compensation for mental agony and harassment, together with the costs of the present complaint;
(c) pass such other and further order(s) as this Commission may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

(Neha Mishra)

Through Counsel

Draft a partnership deed under the Indian Partnership Act, 1932 for the firm 'Krishna Enterprises' at Mumbai, business of wholesale of textiles, between Mr. Gurpreet Bhatia, Ms. Sneha Pandey, Mr. Praveen Sandhu. Capital ₹15,50,000 each, profits/losses in equal shares amongst themselves, with arbitration and dissolution clauses.

DEED OF PARTNERSHIP

This Deed of Partnership is made and executed at Mumbai, Maharashtra, on this 28th day of October, 2026,

BY AND BETWEEN
   (a) Mr. Gurpreet Bhatia, hereinafter referred to as Partner No. 1;
   (b) Ms. Sneha Pandey, hereinafter referred to as Partner No. 2;
   (c) Mr. Praveen Sandhu, hereinafter referred to as Partner No. 3;

The above-named persons are hereinafter collectively referred to as the "Partners" and individually as a "Partner".

WHEREAS the Partners have agreed to carry on business in partnership and are desirous of reducing the terms and conditions of their partnership into writing, in accordance with the provisions of the Indian Partnership Act, 1932.

NOW THIS DEED WITNESSETH AS UNDER:

1. NAME OF THE FIRM — The partnership business shall be carried on in the name and style of "Krishna Enterprises" (the "Firm"), or in such other name as the Partners may mutually decide from time to time.

2. PLACE OF BUSINESS — The principal place of business of the Firm shall be at Mumbai, and/or at such other place or places as the Partners may mutually agree upon.

3. NATURE OF BUSINESS — The business of the Firm shall be that of wholesale of textiles, and/or any other lawful business as may be mutually agreed upon in writing by the Partners.

4. COMMENCEMENT AND DURATION — The partnership shall commence with effect from the date of these presents and shall be a partnership at will.

5. CAPITAL — The capital of the Firm shall be contributed by the Partners in the sum of ₹15,50,000 (Rupees Fifteen Lakh Fifty Thousand only) each, aggregating to ₹46,50,000 (Rupees Forty Six Lakh Fifty Thousand only), and further capital, if required, shall be brought in by the Partners as may be mutually agreed.

6. PROFITS AND LOSSES — The net profits and losses of the Firm, after providing for all working expenses, shall be divided between and borne by the Partners in equal shares amongst themselves.

7. BANK ACCOUNT — The Firm shall maintain a bank account in the name of the Firm, which shall be operated by such of the Partners as may be authorised by mutual consent.

8. BOOKS OF ACCOUNT — Proper books of account shall be kept at the place of business of the Firm and shall be open to inspection by each Partner; the accounts shall be made up and settled at the close of each financial year.

9. MANAGEMENT — Every Partner shall be just and faithful to the other Partners, shall diligently attend to the business of the Firm, and no Partner shall, without the consent of the others, lend the Firm's money or stand surety for any person on behalf of the Firm.

10. RETIREMENT, DEATH AND INSOLVENCY — On the retirement, death or insolvency of any Partner, the Firm shall not be dissolved but shall be continued by the remaining Partners, who shall pay to the outgoing Partner or his legal heirs the amount standing to his credit, as ascertained from the books of the Firm.

11. DISSOLUTION — In the event of dissolution of the Firm, the assets of the Firm shall be realised and applied, and the surplus, if any, shall be distributed among the Partners in proportion to their respective shares, in accordance with the provisions of the Indian Partnership Act, 1932.

12. ARBITRATION — All disputes and differences arising between the Partners touching the affairs of the Firm or the interpretation of this Deed shall be referred to the arbitration of a sole arbitrator appointed by mutual consent, and the decision of such arbitration shall be final and binding on the Partners.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the Partners have set their respective hands to this Deed of Partnership on the day, month and year first hereinabove written.

____________________
(Mr. Gurpreet Bhatia)

____________________
(Ms. Sneha Pandey)

____________________
(Mr. Praveen Sandhu)

WITNESSES:
1. ____________________
2. ____________________

Please draft a Memorandum of Understanding between Ms. Sneha Sheikh of Nagpur (First Party) and Mrs. Vaishali Das of Vadodara (Second Party) for setting up a shared logistics network. Keep it largely non-binding except confidentiality and dispute resolution, valid for 36 months.

MEMORANDUM OF UNDERSTANDING

This Memorandum of Understanding (hereinafter referred to as this "MOU") is made and entered into on this 21st day of November, 2024, at Nagpur,

BY AND BETWEEN

Ms. Sneha Sheikh, residing at Flat No. 35, Pearl Residency, Gandhi Chowk, Nagpur 331105 (hereinafter referred to as the "First Party", which expression shall, unless repugnant to the context, include his/her heirs, executors, administrators and permitted assigns) of the ONE PART;

AND

Mrs. Vaishali Das, residing at Flat No. 68, Sai Residency, Civil Lines, Vadodara 112385 (hereinafter referred to as the "Second Party", which expression shall, unless repugnant to the context, include his/her heirs, executors, administrators and permitted assigns) of the OTHER PART.

The First Party and the Second Party are hereinafter individually referred to as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties".

WHEREAS the First Party is engaged in and possesses experience relevant to the proposed collaboration; AND WHEREAS the Second Party desires to associate with the First Party for the purpose of setting up a shared logistics network; AND WHEREAS the Parties are desirous of recording the broad understanding reached between them by way of this MOU.

NOW, THEREFORE, THE PARTIES HEREBY RECORD THEIR UNDERSTANDING AS UNDER:

1. PURPOSE — The Parties shall, in good faith, explore and undertake the logistics collaboration described above and shall extend to each other reasonable cooperation towards its implementation.

2. SCOPE OF COOPERATION — The Parties shall share such information, resources and expertise as may be mutually agreed in writing from time to time, and shall constitute a joint working committee to oversee the activities contemplated herein.

3. COSTS — Save as otherwise agreed in writing, each Party shall bear its own costs and expenses incurred in connection with this MOU.

4. TERM — This MOU shall come into effect from the date first written above and shall, unless extended or terminated earlier in accordance herewith, remain in force for a period of 36 months.

5. CONFIDENTIALITY — Each Party shall keep confidential all proprietary and non-public information disclosed to it by the other Party and shall not disclose the same to any third party without prior written consent, save as required by law. This obligation of confidentiality shall survive the termination of this MOU.

6. BINDING EFFECT — Save and except the clauses relating to Confidentiality, Dispute Resolution and Governing Law, which the Parties intend to be legally binding, this MOU records the present intention of the Parties only and is not intended to create any legally binding obligation on either Party.

7. TERMINATION — Either Party may terminate this MOU by giving thirty (30) days' prior written notice to the other Party, without assigning any reason, and such termination shall be without prejudice to any accrued rights or obligations of the Parties.

8. DISPUTE RESOLUTION — Any dispute or difference arising out of or in connection with this MOU shall, in the first instance, be resolved amicably through mutual negotiation between the Parties, failing which the same shall be referred to and finally resolved by arbitration by a sole arbitrator to be appointed by mutual consent, the seat of arbitration being Nagpur.

9. GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION — This MOU shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India, and subject to the arbitration clause aforesaid, the courts at Nagpur shall have exclusive jurisdiction.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the Parties have set their respective hands to this MOU on the day, month and year first hereinabove written.

For the First Party                                   For the Second Party


____________________                                  ____________________
(Ms. Sneha Sheikh)                                                (Mrs. Vaishali Das)

WITNESSES:
1. ____________________
2. ____________________

(opposite party) regarding defective refrigerator that was never repaired or replaced despite the warranty. Amount paid ₹6,00,000; claim ₹8,70,000 with refund and compensation.

BEFORE THE DISTRICT CONSUMER DISPUTES REDRESSAL COMMISSION, SURAT, GUJARAT

CONSUMER COMPLAINT NO. ________ OF 2025

(Complaint under Section 35 of the Consumer Protection Act, 2019)

IN THE MATTER OF:

Mrs. Harleen Shah,
residing at Flat No. 21, Sapphire Towers, Andheri West, Surat 467495
                                                                    ... COMPLAINANT

VERSUS

Pinnacle Realtors Pvt. Ltd.,
[REGISTERED OFFICE / BRANCH ADDRESS]
                                                                    ... OPPOSITE PARTY

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

1. That the Complainant is a "consumer" within the meaning of Section 2(7) of the Consumer Protection Act, 2019, having availed of the goods/services of the Opposite Party for valuable consideration, and the present complaint is filed under Section 35 of the said Act.

2. That on 04.07.2026, the Complainant, for consideration of ₹6,00,000 (Rupees Six Lakh only), availed of the goods/services of the Opposite Party in respect of the matter giving rise to the present complaint.

3. FACTS OF THE CASE — That the Complainant's grievance arises from defective refrigerator that was never repaired or replaced despite the warranty. Despite repeated requests and reminders, the Opposite Party failed and neglected to redress the grievance of the Complainant.

4. That the aforesaid acts and omissions on the part of the Opposite Party amount to defective goods and have caused the Complainant financial loss, mental agony and harassment, for which the Opposite Party is liable to compensate the Complainant.

5. CAUSE OF ACTION — That the cause of action for the present complaint arose on 04.07.2026 and on subsequent dates when the Opposite Party failed to redress the grievance, and the cause of action is continuing.

6. LIMITATION — That the present complaint is filed within the period of two years from the date on which the cause of action arose, and is therefore within limitation as prescribed under Section 69 of the Consumer Protection Act, 2019.

7. JURISDICTION — That this Commission has the territorial jurisdiction to entertain and try the present complaint, as the Complainant resides and the cause of action, wholly or in part, arose within its jurisdiction; and the complaint is also within the pecuniary jurisdiction of this Commission, the value of the goods/services and the compensation claimed not exceeding the limit prescribed.

8. That the Complainant has not filed any other complaint or proceeding in respect of the subject matter of the present complaint before any other forum or authority.

PRAYER

It is, therefore, most respectfully prayed that this Commission may be pleased to:
(a) direct the Opposite Party to refund/pay to the Complainant a sum of ₹8,70,000 (Rupees Eight Lakh Seventy Thousand only) on account of the defects in the goods aforesaid;
(b) direct the Opposite Party to pay compensation for mental agony and harassment, together with the costs of the present complaint;
(c) pass such other and further order(s) as this Commission may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.


____________________
COMPLAINANT
(Harleen Shah)

Through Counsel

VERIFICATION

I, Harleen Shah, the Complainant above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 8 and the prayer of the present complaint are true and correct to the best of my knowledge and belief, and that nothing material has been concealed therefrom. Verified at Surat on [DATE].

Draft a partnership deed under the Indian Partnership Act, 1932 for the firm 'Royal Agencies' at Ahmedabad, business of providing IT consultancy services, between Mr. Aditya Kaur, Mr. Mahesh Mukherjee. Capital ₹22,00,000 each, profits/losses in equal shares, with arbitration and dissolution clauses.

DEED OF PARTNERSHIP

This Deed of Partnership is made and executed at Ahmedabad, Gujarat, on this 14th day of December, 2026,

BY AND BETWEEN
   (a) Mr. Aditya Kaur, hereinafter referred to as Partner No. 1;
   (b) Mr. Mahesh Mukherjee, hereinafter referred to as Partner No. 2;

The above-named persons are hereinafter collectively referred to as the "Partners" and individually as a "Partner".

WHEREAS the Partners have agreed to carry on business in partnership and are desirous of reducing the terms and conditions of their partnership into writing, in accordance with the provisions of the Indian Partnership Act, 1932.

NOW THIS DEED WITNESSETH AS UNDER:

1. NAME OF THE FIRM — The partnership business shall be carried on in the name and style of "Royal Agencies" (the "Firm"), or in such other name as the Partners may mutually decide from time to time.

2. PLACE OF BUSINESS — The principal place of business of the Firm shall be at Ahmedabad, and/or at such other place or places as the Partners may mutually agree upon.

3. NATURE OF BUSINESS — The business of the Firm shall be that of providing IT consultancy services, and/or any other lawful business as may be mutually agreed upon in writing by the Partners.

4. COMMENCEMENT AND DURATION — The partnership shall commence with effect from the date of these presents and shall be a partnership at will.

5. CAPITAL — The capital of the Firm shall be contributed by the Partners in the sum of ₹22,00,000 (Rupees Twenty Two Lakh only) each, aggregating to ₹44,00,000 (Rupees Forty Four Lakh only), and further capital, if required, shall be brought in by the Partners as may be mutually agreed.

6. PROFITS AND LOSSES — The net profits and losses of the Firm, after providing for all working expenses, shall be divided between and borne by the Partners in equal shares.

7. BANK ACCOUNT — The Firm shall maintain a bank account in the name of the Firm, which shall be operated by such of the Partners as may be authorised by mutual consent.

8. BOOKS OF ACCOUNT — Proper books of account shall be kept at the place of business of the Firm and shall be open to inspection by each Partner; the accounts shall be made up and settled at the close of each financial year.

9. MANAGEMENT — Every Partner shall be just and faithful to the other Partners, shall diligently attend to the business of the Firm, and no Partner shall, without the consent of the others, lend the Firm's money or stand surety for any person on behalf of the Firm.

10. RETIREMENT, DEATH AND INSOLVENCY — On the retirement, death or insolvency of any Partner, the Firm shall not be dissolved but shall be continued by the remaining Partners, who shall pay to the outgoing Partner or his legal heirs the amount standing to his credit, as ascertained from the books of the Firm.

11. DISSOLUTION — In the event of dissolution of the Firm, the assets of the Firm shall be realised and applied, and the surplus, if any, shall be distributed among the Partners in proportion to their respective shares, in accordance with the provisions of the Indian Partnership Act, 1932.

12. ARBITRATION — All disputes and differences arising between the Partners touching the affairs of the Firm or the interpretation of this Deed shall be referred to the arbitration of a sole arbitrator appointed by mutual consent, and the decision of such arbitration shall be final and binding on the Partners.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the Partners have set their respective hands to this Deed of Partnership on the day, month and year first hereinabove written.

____________________
(Mr. Aditya Kaur)

____________________
(Mr. Mahesh Mukherjee)

WITNESSES:
1. ____________________
2. ____________________

Sir/Madam,

Under instructions from and on behalf of my client, Mrs. Sushma Mishra, residing at Flat No. 2, Shanti Niketan, JM Road, Nashik 472149 (hereinafter "my client"), I address you as under:

1. That a sum of ₹7,70,000 (Rupees Seven Lakh Seventy Thousand only) is due and payable by you to my client towards an advance paid to you for work not performed, the said amount having become due and outstanding as on 07.04.2024.

Draft a Maharashtra leave and license agreement. Licensor is Ms. Zoya Sandhu, Licensee is Ms. Neha Kulkarni. Premises: Office No. 31, Green Acres, Baner, Nagpur. License fee ₹17,500 per month, interest-free refundable deposit ₹3,10,000, term 11 months, one month notice.

LEAVE AND LICENSE AGREEMENT

This Leave and License Agreement is made and executed at Nagpur on this 4th day of October, 2025,

BETWEEN

Ms. Zoya Sandhu, residing at Flat No. 43, Krishna Apartments, Camp, Nagpur 181948 (hereinafter referred to as the "Licensor", which expression shall, unless repugnant to the context, include his/her heirs, legal representatives and permitted assigns) of the ONE PART;

AND

Ms. Neha Kulkarni, an adult, (hereinafter referred to as the "Licensee", which expression shall, unless repugnant to the context, include his/her heirs, legal representatives and permitted assigns) of the OTHER PART.

WHEREAS the Licensor is the absolute owner of and is well and sufficiently entitled to the residential flat being Office No. 31, Green Acres, Baner, Nagpur, more particularly described in the Schedule hereunder (the "said premises"); AND WHEREAS the Licensee has approached the Licensor for permission to use and occupy the said premises on leave and license basis, which the Licensor has agreed to grant on the terms and conditions hereinafter appearing.

NOW THIS AGREEMENT WITNESSETH AS UNDER:

1. GRANT OF LICENSE — The Licensor hereby grants unto the Licensee a leave and license to use and occupy the said premises for the purpose aforesaid, on a purely leave and license basis.

2. TERM — This license is granted for a period of 11 (Eleven) months commencing from the date of these presents, and shall stand automatically determined on the expiry of the said term unless renewed by a fresh agreement in writing.

3. LICENSE FEE — The Licensee shall pay to the Licensor a monthly license fee of ₹17,500 (Rupees Seventeen Thousand Five Hundred only), payable in advance on or before the fifth day of each English calendar month.

4. SECURITY DEPOSIT — The Licensee has, on or before the execution hereof, paid to the Licensor an interest-free refundable security deposit of ₹3,10,000 (Rupees Three Lakh Ten Thousand only), which the Licensor shall refund to the Licensee, free of interest, at the time of handing over vacant and peaceful possession of the said premises, subject to deduction of dues, if any.

5. USE — The Licensee shall use the said premises only for the permitted purpose and shall not carry on therein any unlawful, hazardous or nuisance-causing activity.

6. NO TENANCY — It is expressly agreed and clarified that this Agreement shall not create any tenancy, lease, sub-lease or any other right, title or interest in the said premises in favour of the Licensee, who shall at all times remain a mere licensee, and the juridical possession of the said premises shall always remain with the Licensor.

7. NOTICE / REVOCATION — Either party may revoke or terminate this license by giving to the other one month's prior written notice; on such revocation or on expiry of the term, the Licensee shall hand over vacant and peaceful possession of the said premises to the Licensor.

8. STAMP DUTY AND REGISTRATION — In compliance with the Maharashtra Rent Control Act, 1999, this Agreement shall be registered before the Sub-Registrar of Assurances having jurisdiction, and the stamp duty and registration charges in respect of this Agreement shall be borne and paid by the Licensee.

9. SOCIETY CHARGES AND UTILITIES — The Licensor shall pay the municipal taxes and society maintenance charges, while the Licensee shall bear the charges for electricity and water consumed during the period of license.

SCHEDULE OF THE SAID PREMISES
All that piece and parcel of residential flat bearing Office No. 31, Green Acres, Baner, Nagpur, together with the fixtures and fittings provided therein.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the parties have set their respective hands to this Agreement on the day, month and year first hereinabove written.
